news.title: Don’t rush to raze illegal buildings: West Bengal CM
news.para: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has cautioned the Kolkata Municipal Corporation's buildings department against hasty demolitions without prior notice. He emphasized a careful approach, especially for buildings occupied by economically backward sections, urging adherence to legal procedures and opportunities for regularization of minor violations.

news.title: Chandigarh administration to auction 10 residential sites from June 2
news.para: Chandigarh administration is set to e-auction 10 residential plots ranging from 100 to 502 square yards later this month. Applications and earnest money deposits are open from June 2 to June 23, with reserve prices between Rs 3.3 crore and Rs 16.5 crore. The auction process will conclude by June 29.

news.title: SC pulls up Jaipur development body over delay in action on commercial use of residential areas
news.para: The Supreme Court has severely criticized the Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) for its non-compliance with court directives regarding commercial establishments in residential areas. The JDA failed to appear or submit an affidavit detailing actions taken after surveys, despite ongoing resident complaints. The court emphasized the planning authority's accountability for enforcing town planning norms and protecting residential zones.

news.title: HC limits relief in building violation cases, clears way for DTCP action in Gurugram
news.para: Authorities can now act on numerous building violation cases in Gurgaon after the Punjab and Haryana High Court narrowed a previous restraint order. The court clarified the order applies only to parties involved in litigation, allowing action against others with alleged unauthorized constructions and commercial activities in residential areas. This paves the way for enforcement proceedings.

news.title: Ghaziabad: Consumer forum orders builder to hand over original deed & compensate homebuyer
news.para: A Ghaziabad consumer commission has ordered developers of Shipra Krishna Srishti to hand over the original sale deed of a flat to HDFC within 15 days. The builders failed to provide the document as required by a tripartite agreement, leading to a compensation order of Rs 50,000 for the flat buyer.

news.title: Punjab RERA orders builder to refund charges, secure completion certificates
news.para: Rera has ordered a builder to refund Rs 5.45 lakh in excess maintenance charges and charges for 342 sq ft of excess area to allottees. The authority also mandated the builder to obtain Completion and Occupation Certificates and pay 12% annual interest on sale consideration until compliance.

news.title: Hyderabad: Second land parcel in Raidurg sold at ₹204 crore per acre
news.para: Hyderabad's prime real estate is fetching record prices, with TGIIC securing Rs 204 crore per acre for a Raidurg plot, sold to Vamsiram builders for Rs 1,040 crore. This follows another auction where a similar plot fetched Rs 237 crore per acre. These sales highlight developer confidence and Hyderabad's growth as a business hub.

news.title: Noida: Societies' upkeep bills set to go up with labour code’s higher wages mandate
news.para: New labor codes mandating higher minimum wages are significantly increasing monthly maintenance bills for residents in NCR condominiums. Manpower costs, forming the bulk of budgets, are projected to rise by 20-30%, forcing many societies to revise Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges upwards.

news.title: ED arrests real estate businessman Vikram Wadhwa in ₹645-crore IDFC First Bank 'fraud' case
news.para: The ED has arrested real estate businessman Vikram Wadhwa, the third arrest in a money laundering probe involving Rs 645 crore embezzled from Haryana government, Chandigarh administration, and two schools. Wadhwa allegedly received over Rs 70 crore in crime proceeds and played a key role in layering and concealing laundered funds.

news.title: Unica Capital buys Monaco property asset for $31.4 million
news.para: European firm Unica Capital has acquired Le Montaigne, a Monaco residential property, for €27 million. This deal underscores the strength of the 'ultra prime' property market despite global economic challenges. Unica Capital's CEO Byron Baciocchi stated that Monaco remains a key focus for the company. The Le Montaigne residence will be available for rent at €180,000 per month.

news.title: Delhi: Apex residents' body seeks round table to formulate fire safety SOP
news.para: Following a fatal fire in East Delhi, the apex body of resident welfare associations (RWAs) in Delhi has demanded a round table with government bodies and utility companies. The aim is to create a standard operating procedure for fire safety in residential buildings within 30 days, addressing gaps in enforcement and protocols.

news.title: Property bought for daughter without a formal gift deed isn't unexplained investment: ITAT
news.para: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a property bought for a daughter by her father is not an unexplained investment without a gift deed. The tribunal emphasized that direct bank payments from the father to the seller, along with other evidence, establish the source of funds. This decision highlights that family transactions should consider human probabilities and surrounding circumstances.

news.title: SC asks Karnataka HC to expeditiously decide ED-Embassy East Business Park dispute
news.para: A Bench of Justice Dipankar Datta and Justice Augustine George Masih passed the order while hearing a Special Leave Petition filed by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) against an interim order of the Karnataka High Court in proceedings involving Embassy East Business Park Pvt. Ltd. and others.

news.title: Dalmandi drive gains pace, 112 buildings demolished so far in Varanasi
news.para: Demolition for the Dalmandi road-widening project has resumed, with over 112 structures razed to improve access to Kashi Vishwanath Dham. The project, aiming to widen the 650-meter road to 17.4 meters, has seen over 90 buildings acquired and compensation exceeding ₹50 crore disbursed. Negotiations continue for six mosques in the project's path.

news.title: Why India’s $105 billion GCC opportunity is a turning point for real estate
news.para: India's Global Capability Centres are transforming into innovation hubs, driving significant growth in the real estate sector.  Projected to reach $100-110 billion by 2030, these centres are demanding new cities, formats, and higher quality commercial and residential spaces, reshaping India's urban landscape.

news.title: Odisha housing board set to expand affordable housing with four projects
news.para: Odisha State Housing Board plans four major projects for FY 2026-27, aiming to deliver nearly 6,877 inclusive residential units. These include the Suango PPP project, Ranasinghpur EPC housing, Patrapada housing, and Basanti Colony joint venture. The board is also seeking government land for affordable housing initiatives to address urban housing needs.

news.title: Gurugram: Two missing links force seven km detour to e-way for Vatika commuters
news.para: Thousands of families in Gurugram's Vatika India Next township face connectivity woes due to incomplete sector road sections, a decade after its launch. The missing links, stemming from land acquisition delays, force a 7km detour to the Dwarka Expressway and worsen monsoon waterlogging. Residents demand urgent resolution.

news.title: Suraj Estate's subsidiary acquires development rights for land in Mumbai for ₹75 crore
news.para: Suraj Estate Developers is expanding its Mumbai presence. The company has secured development rights for a prime plot in Mahim, Mumbai. This acquisition is set to significantly boost its commercial project, Suraj One Business Bay. The new development is projected to add substantial saleable area and a considerable gross development value. This strategic move enhances the project's overall worth.

news.title: Puravankara to jointly develop 11.23-acre land in North Bengaluru
news.para: Puravankara has secured an 11.23-acre land parcel in Doddagubbi, North Bengaluru, for a residential project estimated at ₹1,100 crore. This move aligns with the company's robust FY26 performance, which saw sales surge by 55% to ₹7,407 crore. Aiming for ₹11,200 crore in FY27 sales, Puravankara is strategically expanding in high-demand markets.

news.title: NCLAT offers Supreme Housing final chance to settle Canara Bank dues
news.para: Supreme Housing promoters have a final opportunity to settle their debt with Canara Bank. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has ordered a deposit of ₹460 crore by June 13. This is the third chance for the company to resolve its financial obligations. Failure to meet this deadline will allow the insolvency process to proceed.
